steps.title: Read the speed constraint from the problem
steps.content: The problem directly states that $30 \leq x \leq 70$ represents the speed constraint. This is a compound inequality: the speed $x$ must be at least 30 mph and at most 70 mph.

steps.title: Find the matching choice
steps.content:
Compare each choice to the constraint $30 \leq x \leq 70$:
  • Choice A — $x \geq 30$: only the lower bound; omits the upper limit
  • Choice B — $x \geq 70$: wrong; this says speed is at least 70, contradicting the problem
  • Choice C — $30 \leq x \leq 70$: exact match
  • Choice D — $x \leq 100$: an upper bound not given in the problem
That confirms C is correct.
